focus: maintainability — DB/operator ownership, generation retention, vector operability, migration/cutover ergonomics, shared command authority, current-state documentation, and fixture realism

findingDetails.title: A worker crash permanently strands a vector job in running
findingDetails.detail: A process exit after claim leaves the vector job permanently unselectable in normal operation.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The only normal drain selects pending/failed jobs; no lease or stale-running reconciliation exists.

findingDetails.title: Persistent queries ignore an explicitly requested generation
findingDetails.detail: The persistent query path silently substitutes the current generation for a caller-supplied generationHash.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Generation pin enforcement exists for cursors and hydration but not direct persistent queries.

findingDetails.title: The generation pointer accepts a symlinked database outside its publication directory
findingDetails.detail: A basename-only pointer can resolve through a symlink outside the generation directory and is not bound to its declared generation hash.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The single pointer resolver feeds persistent query and hydration; no reader-side realpath containment or hash binding exists.

findingDetails.title: Persistent retrieval accepts arbitrarily large query vectors
findingDetails.detail: The synchronous persistent query path accepts and duplicates an unbounded caller-controlled queryVector.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Query-vector validation checks finite/non-empty values only; the embedder-output cap does not protect query inputs.

findingDetails.title: The aggregate hash includes the mutable slug locator
findingDetails.detail: Artifact relativePath includes slug and is hashed, so locator-only renames rotate generation identity.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The aggregate-hash producer and artifact-path producer were traced; retrieval hash remains separate.

findingDetails.title: Phase 001 marks a ten-iteration research task complete after only seven iterations
findingDetails.detail: Phase 001 marks a ten-iteration research task complete even though the authoritative handoff records only seven iterations and current strict validation fails.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Spec, tasks, checklist, synthesis, handoff, and parent map were reconciled; only checked completion evidence asserts ten executed iterations.

adjudication.claim: Checked ten-iteration execution contradicts the seven-iteration authoritative handoff.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Promoted synthesis and lineage artifact roster for omitted iterations.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Ten may have been a maximum, but T002 claims execution and the handoff records a stall.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Correct checked rows and cite a legal seven-iteration convergence stop.

findingDetails.title: Phase 002 checks both research lineages complete although GLM produced zero iterations
findingDetails.detail: Phase 002 marks both SOL and GLM lineages complete and converged although GLM produced zero iterations and the synthesis is SOL-only.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Spec, tasks, checklist, SOL synthesis, handoff, and parent map agree on SOL=20/GLM=0 except checked completion rows.

adjudication.claim: Both lineages are checked complete despite GLM failing before iteration one.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: GLM delta, dissent, or successful secondary iteration in synthesis and handoff.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: SOL may be sufficient, but the frozen requirement explicitly requires both lineages.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Amend requirements and checked rows or provide real GLM merge evidence.

findingDetails.title: Phase 003 claims a corpus-scale SLO was measured from a 20-style fixture
findingDetails.detail: Phase 003 marks the corpus-scale persistent-query SLO measured and proven although its test fixture contains only twenty styles and the handoff defers the full-corpus check.
findingDetails.scopeProof: REQ-007, tasks, checklist, the sole timing test, and handoff limitations were traced; no 1,290-style persistent benchmark exists.

adjudication.claim: A 20-style fixture is presented as proof against the same-corpus 1,290-style baseline.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Exact SLO/timing search and all 24 current DB tests.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: The bounded test is useful regression evidence but not same-corpus proof.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Run and record the same full-corpus comparison or correct/defer the checked SLO claim.

findingDetails.title: The parent declares the four-phase program complete and verified while required evidence is false
findingDetails.detail: The parent declares all four phases complete and verified despite false checked research/SLO evidence, active required defects, and a failing current recursive strict validation.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parent handoff criteria were reconciled against all four child matrices, current tests/checkers, strict validation, and the corrected active baseline.

adjudication.claim: Parent Complete is unsupported while mandatory child evidence is contradicted and active P1s remain.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Current DB, legacy, command tests/checkers, and recursive strict validation.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Complete may mean delivered, but the parent explicitly claims built and verified.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Reconcile child evidence/defects and strict validation or narrow the parent lifecycle claim.

findingDetails.title: Persistent DB maintenance has no owned operator surface or bounded generation-retention path
findingDetails.detail: Full builds retain uniquely named immutable generations, while publication, rollback, vector repair, and queue drain are library-only with no production operator owner or prune policy.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found the maintenance exports consumed only internally or by tests; CLI has legacy build/query/hydrate only and generation deletion targets temporary/building paths.

adjudication.claim: Persistent DB publication and repair are library-only surfaces, and repeated full builds retain uniquely named generation files without a bounded retention or prune path.
adjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched all styles modules/tests and packet docs for production consumers, operator commands, generation pruning, retention policy, or a runbook; none was found.
adjudication.alternativeExplanation: Deployments can script the callable exports and legacy remains default, but this supplies neither a stable tested maintenance owner nor bounded file retention.
adjudication.downgradeTrigger: Provide a documented/tested operator surface and explicit safe retention/pruning invariant.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Prior evidence-backed protocol failure carried; exhausted protocol was not re-run in maintainability.
Prior false checked evidence remains active; exhausted protocol was not re-run in maintainability.

noveltyJustification: One distinct new P1 adds weight 5 to the prior active weight 41; no refinement weight was claimed and prior crash/SLO findings were not duplicated.
edgeCases: Legacy default contains immediate operational risk.
Rollback requires retaining at least one prior generation; absence of a bounded keep/prune invariant is the defect.
Fixture-scale SLO gap is already represented by SOL-I003-P1-003 and was not duplicated.
Structural graph unavailable; graphless direct evidence used.
Unrelated dirty workspace paths were not modified.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Persistent DB publication/repair spans storage, vector jobs, adapter CLI, operator docs, shared commands, and packet current-state claims.

selectedTargets: DB generation publication, rollback, and retention
adapter and production CLI cutover surface
shared creation contract and canonical command authority
targetSelection.selectionReason: Strategy Next Focus and rendered prompt prioritize maintenance ownership, cleanup/rollback, vector operability, shared-command change cost, current-state discipline, and fixtures.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Persistent publication and maintenance have one owned, test-covered operator surface and bounded generation retention.

searchActions.result: Exports are internal/test-only; CLI has no persistent maintenance commands; only temporary/building files are deleted.

searchLedger.rationale: Operational ownership and retention are absent across all maintenance consumers.

searchLedger.invariant: Canonical wrappers reference one lifecycle authority rather than copying shared choreography or taste.

searchActions.queryOrPath: shared contract and canonical interface router
searchActions.result: Lifecycle/proof/mutation/failure contracts are centralized; wrapper owns only discrimination, assets, and mode-specific summary.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Central authority and thin-router boundary are explicit.
searchLedger.rationale: No materially duplicated shared authority was found.

searchLedger.invariant: Operator docs consistently identify the supported default and opt-in mode.

searchActions.queryOrPath: README, adapter, skill docs, changelog, phase handoff
searchActions.result: All checked surfaces identify legacy as default, flat files as authoritative, and persistent as explicit opt-in.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Small fixtures create a new maintainability finding beyond the active SLO evidence gap.

searchActions.result: Fixtures are suitable for lifecycle tests; corpus-scale evidence gap is already SOL-I003-P1-003.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: A new finding would duplicate the active SLO evidence finding.
searchLedger.rationale: No distinct fixture-maintenance defect was established.

evidence: Exact searches and direct reads used; iteration did not block on structural-impact analysis.

reason: Shared lifecycle is centralized and canonical command is a thin router.
README, adapter, skill docs, changelog, and handoff consistently identify legacy as default.
A new finding would duplicate active SOL-I003-P1-003.
